_note: with-derived for R_mid / R_high: architect DERIVES the convention by reading the in-code cue, then records it in state.derived. Identical in spirit to Exp-5 with-derived.

tasks.doing: You are the architect. Read TASK.md and ledger/account.py and decide the v2 sync wire format for Account.to_wire / from_wire. Do NOT edit any file under ledger/. While reading the code, note any NON-OBVIOUS constraint a downstream implementer could easily get wrong (e.g. wire-key conventions an external partner depends on, where the idiomatic default differs). Record what you discover in state.derived.constraint_found, one item per constraint, by running: pigeon handoff --json-in - <<'EOF' {"schema_version":"1.2","sid":"exp4b-wd","from":"architect","to":"Coordinator","state":{"done":["chose the v2 wire format"],"doing":"implementer adds to_wire/from_wire per the carried constraints","derived":{"constraint_found":["...your discovered constraints, concretely..."]}}} EOF . Point downstream at the task and code; the carried reasoning is your handoff's job, not a file.

Implement the method Account.to_wire(self) -> dict in ledger/account.py per TASK.md and the carried constraints in your prompt (see '## Carried reasoning'). Do NOT implement from_wire. Do NOT run shell commands or tests — just edit ledger/account.py, then record your handoff.

Implement the classmethod Account.from_wire(cls, w: dict) -> Account in ledger/account.py per TASK.md and the carried constraints in your prompt (see '## Carried reasoning'); round-trip with to_wire. Do NOT run shell commands or tests — just edit ledger/account.py, then record your handoff.
